page: Amazon Web Services vt1.3xlarge is a specialized compute-focused instance designed for video transcoding workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, this instance features 12 dedicated vCPUs across 6 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The CPU architecture includes support for Advanced Vector Extensions, specifically AVX, AVX512F, and AVX512_VNNI, alongside cryptography acceleration via AES. It is equipped with 24.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core.

Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities across general compute and memory operations. The instance achieved a multi-core stress-ng CPU score of 10177.46 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 5969.0, both placing in the middle tier of tested hardware. Single-core performance is relatively weak, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1324.09 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1230.0. Memory bandwidth is average, reaching 72.07 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 44.54 GB/sec for large-block write operations.

The vt1.3xlarge does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 3 Gbps. In terms of specialized workloads, the instance demonstrates average performance in static web serving at 8.73 GB/sec and Redis SET operations at 9.5 million operations per second. However, it exhibits poor performance for medium-sized language model inference, processing only 14.1 tokens per second for a 7B model.

Given its hardware profile, the vt1.3xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that benefit from dedicated Intel Xeon cores and AVX-512 vector instructions without requiring massive memory footprints or local SSD storage. It is best suited for video transcoding, software compilation, and general-purpose multi-threaded processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services vt1.3xlarge is a compute-focused instance optimized for video transcoding and multi-threaded processing. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor with 12 dedicated vCPUs and 24.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on a 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results show average multi-core performance, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 5969.0 and a multi-core stress-ng score of 10177.46, though single-core performance is relatively weak. The hardware supports AVX-512 vector instructions, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-bound tasks that do not require high memory-to-CPU ratios. It is best suited for video transcoding, software compilation, and moderate web serving workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services vt1.3xlarge is a compute-focused instance featuring 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 24.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 3 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for video transcoding.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services vt1.3xlarge is an Intel-powered instance with 12 vCPUs and 24 GB RAM, designed for video transcoding and compute tasks.
tagline: A dedicated Intel Xeon compute instance with AVX-512 support, optimized for video transcoding and multi-threaded workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: 12 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor
24.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
AVX-512 vector instruction support for accelerated processing
3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Optimized for video transcoding, software compilation, and web serving
